current_official.title: Artemis III (current official mission)
current_official.mission_type: Rendezvous and Docking in Low Earth Orbit
current_official.summary: NASA now defines Artemis III as a low Earth orbit integrated operations test between Orion and one or both commercial landers from SpaceX and Blue Origin. NASA says specific mission design details will be announced closer to launch.
known: Launch on SLS with crew in Orion
Tests rendezvous and docking with one or both commercial landers
unknown: No public altitude / inclination / phasing profile
No public phase-by-phase timeline beyond rendezvous/docking demo

legacy_lunar_profile.title: Artemis III (archived lunar landing profile)
legacy_lunar_profile.summary: Archived NASA pages still document the earlier Artemis III lunar south-pole mission concept. This is the only publicly detailed Artemis III flight path NASA has published at phase level.

orion_staging_orbit.name: Southern 9:2 NRHO
notes: Chosen for South Pole access and low maintenance cost
Gateway baseline orbit is the same family

phases.label: Ascent staging
phases.summary: Booster/fairing/LAS jettison, then core stage shutdown and separation.

phases.label: Temporary Earth orbit
phases.summary: Enter Earth orbit, perform systems checks and solar panel adjustments.

phases.label: Translunar injection
phases.summary: Depart Earth orbit for the Moon.

phases.label: Outbound translunar cruise
phases.summary: Several outbound correction burns on the way to the Moon.

phases.label: Outbound powered flyby
phases.summary: Lunar flyby maneuver near the Moon.

phases.label: NRHO insertion
phases.summary: Insert into lunar near-rectilinear halo orbit.

phases.label: Lander ready in NRHO
phases.summary: HLS has already reached NRHO before crew arrival in the archived concept.

phases.label: Dock / crew transfer
phases.summary: Orion docks to HLS; two crew transfer.

phases.label: Descent staging
phases.summary: Undock, depart NRHO, and head toward low lunar orbit / descent.

phases.label: Surface mission
phases.summary: Approx. week-long surface mission near lunar South Pole.

phases.label: Orion loiter
phases.summary: Orion remains in NRHO for about one orbit (~6.5 days).

phases.label: Ascent / rendezvous
phases.summary: Ascend from the surface, rendezvous, and dock back with Orion.

phases.label: Post-docking loiter
phases.summary: Up to five days in lunar orbit preparing for return.

phases.label: NRHO departure
phases.summary: Depart NRHO at the optimal point for Earth return.

phases.label: Return powered flyby
phases.summary: Powered flyby near the Moon to target Earth return.

phases.label: Service module separation
phases.summary: Crew module separates before atmospheric entry.
